metadata.description: Optional profile / policy metadata. `risk_level` hints the intended security posture â€” `permissive` triggers a build-time warning so operators do not accidentally ship a loose policy to production.

risk_level.description: Intended posture: strict, balanced, or permissive. Build warns on permissive and can be hard-failed with --fail-on-permissive.

allowed_hosts.description: Optional Host header allowlist. When set, requests whose Host does not match any entry are rejected at the edge. Entries support exact match (api.example.com) and wildcard prefix (*.example.com).

trust_forwarded_for.description: If false (default), any client-supplied X-Forwarded-For header is stripped before the request reaches origin auth / logging. Enable only when the edge sits behind a trusted upstream proxy that you intentionally want to honor.

max_query_length.description: Maximum querystring length in bytes. Requests exceeding this are rejected at the edge.

max_query_params.description: Maximum number of querystring keys. Protects against HTTP parameter pollution and cache poisoning.

max_uri_length.description: Maximum request URI (path + query) length in bytes.

max_header_size.description: Maximum individual request header value size in bytes.

max_header_count.description: Maximum number of request headers allowed at the edge. Protects origin parsers from hash-collision / amplification under small-but-many-headers payloads. Exceeding this returns 431 (Request Header Fields Too Large). Default 64.

oneOf.description: Legacy shape: list of literal substrings. Regex-style entries are rejected at build time; use the object form instead.
Typed shape: literal substrings under contains, RegExp sources under regex (compiled at build time).

allowed_algorithms.description: Optional whitelist of JWT header `alg` values accepted at verify time. If omitted, only the gate's configured `algorithm` is accepted. `none` is always rejected.

clock_skew_sec.description: Tolerance (in seconds) applied to JWT exp/nbf checks. Default 30.

cache_ttl_sec.description: TTL (seconds) for cached gate material (for example, JWKS responses). Bounded to 24h to force rotation windows.

exact_path.description: If true, signed-URL gates require the request URI to exactly match a protected prefix. If false (default, back-compat), any URI under the prefix is acceptable â€” which lets an attacker replay a signature issued for one sibling path against another. Archetypes for sensitive downloads should always set `true`.

nonce_param.description: Optional query-parameter name carrying a per-URL nonce (e.g. `nonce`). When set, the edge forwards the nonce to origin as `X-Signed-URL-Nonce` so origin can enforce single-use (SET NX in Redis / KV). The edge alone cannot enforce replay protection â€” origin cooperation is required. See docs/signed-urls.md.

csp_report_only.description: CSP value to emit in Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header for staged rollout. Pair with csp_report_uri. Browsers will warn but not block violations â€” use to validate policy before setting csp_public/csp_admin.

csp_report_uri.description: URL for CSP violation reports. Emitted as `report-uri` directive if set. Prefer report-to + Reporting-Endpoints for modern browsers.

csp_nonce.description: Generate a per-response nonce and substitute `'nonce-PLACEHOLDER'` in csp_public / csp_admin with the value. The nonce is also exposed as `x-csp-nonce` response header so origin templates can reference it. Cloudflare uses crypto.getRandomValues; CFF falls back to Math.random (lower entropy; documented).

coop.description: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy. Together with COEP, isolates the browsing context and re-enables SharedArrayBuffer / high-precision timers. `same-origin` is the strict default.

coep.description: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy. `require-corp` blocks embedding of any cross-origin resource that does not explicitly opt in via CORP. `credentialless` is a softer alternative for third-party subresources.

corp.description: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy. Controls whether other origins may embed this resource.

reporting_endpoints.description: Value for the Reporting-Endpoints header (e.g., `csp-endpoint="https://example.com/csp"`). Used with `report-to` directive in CSP.

force_vary_auth.description: When true (default), every path covered by any auth gate receives `Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate` + `Vary: Authorization, Cookie` at viewer-response. Prevents CDN edge caches from serving one user's authenticated response to another. Set false only if you understand the cache-poisoning risk (e.g., all protected responses are user-independent, such as prerendered public dashboards).

clear_site_data_paths.description: Paths on which to emit `Clear-Site-Data: "cache", "cookies", "storage"` + force `Cache-Control: no-store`. Use for logout / session-invalidation endpoints so the browser drops cached data even if the origin response is cached. Entries are prefix-matched (match when URI equals or starts with prefix + `/`). Only applied on 2xx and 3xx responses to avoid clobbering failed logouts.

clear_site_data_types.description: Override the default directive set for Clear-Site-Data. Default: `["cache","cookies","storage"]`. `executionContexts` forces the browser to reload the top-level frame and every same-origin frame.

max_age.description: CORS preflight cache duration in seconds. Most browsers cap this at 86400 (Chrome) or 7200 (Firefox).

rate_limit.description: Per-IP request ceiling over the 5-minute WAF rate-based window. AWS WAFv2 accepts 100..2,000,000,000. Legacy single global rule; prefer `rate_limit_rules` for per-route / per-key controls.

rate_limit_rules.description: Fine-grained rate-limit rules. Each entry renders as one `aws_wafv2_rule_group` rule with its own `rate_based_statement` and optional `scope_down_statement` so you can scope by URI, auth state, or header keys without hand-editing Terraform. `limit` is per 5-minute window (AWS WAFv2 accepts 100..2,000,000,000). `aggregate_key_type` defaults to `IP`; `FORWARDED_IP` follows the `X-Forwarded-For` chain and `CUSTOM_KEYS` lets you pass a raw `custom_keys` block. `scope_down_statement` is forwarded verbatim as a raw JSON object â€” schema-free because AWS WAFv2 ships dozens of statement kinds; lint only checks the shell.

managed_rules.description: AWS WAFv2 managed rule group names (vendor: AWS). Common pick-list: AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet (core OWASP Top 10), AWSManagedRulesKnownBadInputsRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesSQLiRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesLinuxRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesUnixRuleSet, AWSManagedRulesBotControlRuleSet (bot scoring, paid), AWSManagedRulesATPRuleSet (account-takeover, paid, scope_down to /login recommended), AWSManagedRulesIPReputationList (reputation-blocked), AWSManagedRulesAnonymousIpList (Tor / open proxies). Lint warns when defaults.mode == enforce and none of BotControl / ATP / IPReputation / AnonymousIp appear in the array. Entries not in the recommended set still compile â€” free-form string is intentional so you can opt into vendor additions without a framework release.

block_response.description: Custom response body attached to every WAF rule whose action is `block`. AWS WAFv2 uses `custom_response_bodies` on the web ACL and references it with `custom_response_body_key` on the block action. Set `status_code` to override the default 403. When omitted, AWS WAFv2 returns its own 403 page (which leaks the CDN vendor).

logging.description: WAF logging configuration. When `enabled: true`, the compiler emits an `aws_wafv2_logging_configuration` block targeting `destination_arn_env` (env-backed Terraform variable reference). `redacted_fields` drops sensitive request fields from the log record â€” `authorization`, `cookie`, `x-api-key`, and `set-cookie` are the usual candidates. Lint warns when `scope == CLOUDFRONT` and logging is disabled because compliance frameworks (PCI-DSS, SOC2) require WAF log retention.

jwks.description: JWKS outbound-fetch guardrails. When `allowed_hosts` is set, the build refuses any JWT gate whose `jwks_url` hostname is not on the list, preventing SSRF into internal metadata endpoints. Regardless of this setting, the compiler also rejects non-HTTPS URLs and URLs that resolve to loopback, private, or link-local literals.

allowed_hosts.description: Exact-match hostname allowlist for any `routes[].auth_gate.jwks_url`. Comparison is case-insensitive. Wildcards are not supported â€” list each IdP host explicitly.

stale_if_error_sec.description: Window (seconds, default 3600, max 86400) to keep serving an expired JWKS cache entry if a refresh fails. Protects against IdP outages causing 100% auth failure. Set to 0 to disable.

negative_cache_sec.description: TTL (seconds, default 60, max 600) for caching JWKS fetch failures to avoid hammering a broken IdP with retries. Set to 0 to disable.

connect.description: Origin connect timeout in seconds. CloudFront caps this at 10s.

read.description: Origin read/response timeout in seconds. CloudFront caps this at 60s.
